candidate.objective: Experienced pipeline construction supervisor with a strong background in poly welding, civil operations, and safety management. Demonstrated capability in leading multi-disciplinary site crews, enforcing WHS compliance, and delivering project outcomes across dewatering, pipeline, and civil construction environments in Western Australia.

duties: Conducting regular inspections of construction sites to identify and eliminate potential safety hazards.
Supervising and directing the pipeline construction team and subcontractors to ensure works are completed safely and to specification.
Educating site personnel on construction safety regulations and accident reporting protocols.
Enforcing site safety rules to minimise the risk of work-related accidents and injuries.
Managing site incidents in accordance with established incident reporting and response procedures.
Maintaining accurate records of construction employee attendance and performance.
Evaluating employee performance and instituting disciplinary measures as required.
Analysing project plans to ensure construction activities meet design, safety, and budget specifications.
Recommending changes to construction operations and procedures to improve efficiency.
Applying sound knowledge of construction safety regulations and demonstrating outstanding leadership skills.
Operating an IT loader to load and unload pipe in accordance with operational specifications.
Completing earthworks to project specifications using all required machine attachments.
Working collaboratively with crews to meet strict project deadlines in a safe and efficient manner.
Completing quality assurance checks to company specifications.
Operating poly welding machines including McElroy, Dixon, and Ritmo across various pipe diameters.
Following engineering plans to complete fabrication works.
Assisting the HSE Advisor with safety audits, review of safe working methods, and team-based risk assessments (TBRA), including serving as subject matter expert for poly welding and plant operations.
Reviewing and auditing company Safe Work Procedures (SWPs) and making relevant updates.
Performing job front inspections and discussing improvement opportunities with personnel.
Conducting pre-start meetings, emergency drills, and toolbox presentations.
Entering data into the safety and compliance matrix and ensuring all KPIs are met.
Leading a team of six staff members across autobody refinishing and custom paintwork operations.
Managing stock and liaising with suppliers to ensure materials availability.
Performing colour matching, full vehicle restoration, and panel spraying to manufacturer specifications.
Processing invoicing and customer transactions.
Ensuring quality assurance across all completed works.

skills.detail: Extensive experience in poly welding, pipeline installation, commissioning, and dewatering system operations across mining and civil construction environments.
Proficient in WHS legislation, site safety enforcement, risk assessment (TBRA), incident management, and safe work procedure development and auditing.
Competent in the operation of IT loaders, articulated dump trucks, and elevated work platforms (EWP).
Strong leadership skills with demonstrated ability to supervise multi-trade construction crews, manage performance, and deliver projects to schedule and budget.

certifications.details: Certified poly welder with experience using McElroy, Dixon, and Ritmo machines across multiple pipe diameters.
Authorised Appointed Person under Schedule 26 for site safety management.
Appointed Person under Section 44 for emergency fire management.
Certified operator of integrated tool carrier (IT) loaders.
Certified operator of articulated dump trucks.
Certified operator of boom-type elevated work platforms.

certifications.ticket: Certificate IV in Occupational Health & Safety

certifications.details: Current Western Australian C Class Drivers Licence.

referees: Professional references available upon request. Please contact ACM Resources to facilitate introduction.
